summary: Applied the control-plane lane policy live by verifying the governed SSH, API, message, and event surfaces currently in use on the Proxmox host and docker-runtime-lv3.

verification.observed: A short-lived step-ca ops SSH certificate successfully authenticated to ops@100.118.189.95 with sudo available and root still restricted to without-password.
The same short-lived ops certificate successfully reached docker-runtime-lv3 over the approved Proxmox jump path.

step-ca returned status ok on https://100.118.189.95:9443/health, the Proxmox API version endpoint returned 9.1.6 for lv3-automation@pve!primary, and the private mail gateway health endpoint returned status ok on docker-runtime-lv3.

docker-runtime-lv3 is listening on TCP 587, the Proxmox sendmail endpoint remains lv3-ops-email to operator@example.com, and the mail gateway OpenAPI contract still includes /webhooks/stalwart as the governed event sink.

notes: The live verification used the current step-ca-backed routine SSH path rather than relying on the older bootstrap key alone.
The event-lane verification checked the documented webhook contract without replaying a live mail event.
